claim: Known findings only; undiscovered defects remain possible.

findings.exploit: A permissive generic payload can bypass kind-specific schema intent.
findings.correction: Exhaustive v0.6 kind-to-Pydantic-to-schema registry with immutable schema digests.

findings.exploit: A projected object can lack an authenticated source identity or exact raw pointer chain.
findings.correction: Typed reconstruction exists; API persistence and authoritative revalidation of the complete source chain remain open.

findings.exploit: Current wall time or later key expiry can invalidate or admit the wrong historical statement.
findings.correction: Trusted signing-time evaluation and explicit revocation/compromise semantics.

findings.exploit: A local CAS link or tenant path can redirect authoritative bytes.
findings.correction: Tenant-bound S3 keys, digest recheck, and link-rejected read-only legacy roots.

findings.exploit: Local child processes can escape incomplete dependency and descendant accounting.
findings.correction: No API/worker subprocess path and lease-bound receipt validation exist; mTLS lease transport and deployed identity checks remain open.

findings.exploit: A witness can invent food, resources, supply, or independence and raise status.
findings.correction: The typed audit kernel exists; authoritative reads must still recompute every DSSE and quorum chain before admitting its inputs.

findings.exploit: Perturbation replay can report baseline science after removing its support.
findings.correction: Object-removal replay creates a new basis and shared-kernel audit; expiry, revocation, and value-modification perturbations remain open.

findings.exploit: Unsafe or inferior candidates can be selected by count-based or incorrectly directed dominance.
findings.correction: Hard filtering, semantic deduplication, Pareto directions, overflow unknown, and four-outcome trees exist; full-kernel successor recomputation remains open.

findings.exploit: Unsigned routing or missing exposure events can fabricate coordination integrity.
findings.correction: Hash-chain and state-machine validation exist; per-event DSSE admission and authenticated routing APIs remain open.

findings.exploit: An unfavorable duplicate primary result can be rejected instead of retained as contradiction.
findings.correction: Duplicate-result and binding contradictions are retained; registration and amendment DSSE quorum recomputation remains open.

findings.exploit: A first-time agent receives optimistic commands that do not reflect workspace authority state.
findings.correction: The response envelope is stable, but onboarding still returns a conservative static blocker set rather than live subsystem aggregation.

findings.exploit: The declared Python range cannot be universally resolved and CI checks stale modules.
findings.correction: uv 0.11.28, Python 3.14.6, and a universal lock are implemented; the v0.6 coverage and security-tool matrix gates remain open.

findings.exploit: A caller can reuse one idempotency key for a different mutation and receive an unrelated cached response.
findings.correction: Bind cached responses to a canonical method, path, tenant, subject, expected-generation, and body digest; full transaction coupling remains open.

findings.exploit: A first-time client cannot inspect the public immutable schema registry without first provisioning a bearer token.
findings.correction: Permit unauthenticated CLI requests only for the public schema registry while retaining authentication on tenant operations.

findings.exploit: A clean source checkout can omit the reviewed universal lock and resolve a different dependency graph.
findings.correction: Remove the uv.lock ignore rule and ignore only ephemeral verification exports.
